steps.assistant_text: bootup_uso object constructor (alloc-or-given a0, init 0x50-byte struct). RE-CLASSIFY-THEN-LEVER: the prior NM-wrap (93.8%) diagnosed an 'unreachable two-instruction scheduling tie' between the s4 store and the four zero/const stores. Re-classifying the diff WORDS showed the real issue was the s5 store being ordered LAST (after the zero/const block) in the C; IDO's list scheduler then deferred the sp-loaded s4 store past the cheap zero-stores. Lever (store-order): move the `a0->0x34 = s5;` statement up to be adjacent to `a0->0x30 = s4;` so both sp-loaded stores stay together ahead of the zero/const block. Result: 0 non-reloc diffs vs expected; reloc symbol set = func_00000000 x2 + D_0000731C HI/LO (matches the .s placeholder callees). The a0+0x28 `&D_00000000` store materialises as `lui 0; addiu 0` post-link, byte-identical to the target's reloc-stripped `lui (0x0>>16); addiu 0x0`. Full `make` ROM stays byte-identical to baserom; verify + verify-blocks pass.

metadata.verification: 0 non-reloc diffs vs expected/.o (reloc-filtered word-compare); reloc symbol set matches .s placeholder callees + D_0000731C; a0+0x28 &D_00000000 resolves to lui0/addiu0 = target bytes; full `make` ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp clean); make verify + verify-blocks pass.
